items.text: Rank-balanced scoring combines complaint-intensity and lower-income-priority percentile ranks.
Magnitude-sensitive scoring combines complaint-intensity and lower-income-priority z-scores.
Every supported scenario scores the constant 2,093-tract candidate universe and selects the top K.
Recorded administrative closure does not prove physical issue resolution.
The workload replay uses actual 2016 30-day arrival cohorts and recorded closure survival by request age.
Workload intervals combine empirical resampling of complete 30-day arrival periods with a Jeffreys beta posterior for recorded-closure probability.
raw_acs_rows = 74002
raw_tiger_tract_rows = 4918
acs_rows_after_identifier_row_removed = 74001
nyc_acs_tract_rows_before_tiger_merge = 2167

In Noise, the Q1-to-Q5 mean tract-intensity ratio is 1.323.
In Housing & Building, the Q1-to-Q5 mean tract-intensity ratio is 3.029.
In Sanitation & Environmental, the Q1-to-Q5 mean tract-intensity ratio is 0.562.
In Street & Infrastructure, the Q1-to-Q5 mean tract-intensity ratio is 0.548.
In Public Safety & Quality of Life, the Q1-to-Q5 mean tract-intensity ratio is 0.459.
The analysis reflects 2016 conditions and is not a current NYC operational recommendation.
311 records reflect both underlying conditions and reporting behavior. Complaint activity is not a direct measurement of true need.
Closed Date records administrative closure and does not necessarily mean the physical condition was fully resolved.

Every tract-domain pair is retained, including zero-request and low-sample combinations. Tract-specific closure curves and workload replay are suppressed below 30 known timing outcomes. The product does not silently treat missing or sparse closure evidence as a zero closure rate.
Approximately 0.5% of matched requests have a Closed Date earlier than Created Date. They are excluded from closure-time measures; anomalies are concentrated in Street & Infrastructure.
The workload model uses actual 30-day arrival periods from one year. It describes the 2016 period pattern, not stable seasonality or a future arrival forecast.
Open at 180 days means the surviving portion of one 30-day arrival cohort when requests reach 180 days old. It is not 180 days of accumulated new arrivals.
Intervals reflect within-2016 period variation and uncertainty in recorded-closure rates. They do not capture future structural changes.
The final 2016 arrival period contains six days. It is shown in the historical pattern but is excluded from full-period resampling.
Standard 311 housing data do not fully represent public-housing in-unit repair workflows.
The analysis identifies descriptive patterns, selection tradeoffs, and assumption-based projections. It does not estimate causal effects.
K is the number of tracts in an analytical selection scenario. It is not staffing, budget, inspections, cases, or intervention capacity.
Tracts with fewer than 500 residents remain visible but are excluded from prioritization because small denominators can create unstable per-capita rates.
Six population-eligible tracts lack the median household income required by the two-component score and remain visible but ineligible.
Rank-balanced percentile scaling and Magnitude-sensitive z-score scaling can produce materially different selected sets.
Census-tract summaries can obscure variation within a tract.
